title: OpenRegs — Federal Regulatory Data
description_html:
Federal regulatory data from the Federal Register API and Regulations.gov. Includes Federal Register documents, regulatory dockets, documents, and public comments with full-text search.

Built by DataDawn.

federal_register.description_html: All Federal Register documents (rules, proposed rules, notices, presidential documents) from 1994 to present. Nearly 1M documents with full-text search.

dockets.description_html: Regulatory dockets from Regulations.gov — containers for related documents and comments in a rulemaking proceeding.

documents.description_html: Regulatory documents from Regulations.gov including rules, proposed rules, notices, and supporting materials.

presidential_documents.description_html: Executive Orders and Proclamations from the Federal Register. Covers EO 12890–14389 (1994–present) with signing dates, EO numbers, and links to full text.

spending_awards.description_html: Federal grants and contracts from USAspending.gov across 20 agencies. 863,000+ awards with amounts, recipients, descriptions, and program codes.

legislation.description_html: Congressional bills and resolutions from Congress.gov, filtered to policy areas relevant to environmental, health, agriculture, and wildlife regulation.

legislation_actions.description_html: Legislative action history for tracked bills — committee referrals, floor votes, amendments, and presidential signatures.

legislation_subjects.description_html: CRS legislative subject terms assigned to bills, useful for filtering by topic.

comments.description_html: Public comments submitted to federal agencies via Regulations.gov. Over 3.9M comments with submitter names and types.

agencies.description_html: Federal agencies from the Federal Register, including parent-child relationships.

agency_map.description_html: Maps Regulations.gov agency codes (EPA, FDA, etc.) to Federal Register agency records.

fr_regs_crossref.description_html: Cross-reference linking Federal Register document numbers to Regulations.gov document IDs.

docket_stats.description_html: Pre-computed view: comment and document counts per docket with date ranges.

comments_by_year.description_html: Pre-computed view: annual comment volume per agency with submitter type breakdown.

fr_by_year.description_html: Pre-computed view: Federal Register document counts by year and type.

top_dockets.description_html: Pre-computed view: dockets ranked by comment volume with submitter breakdown.

regulatory_pipeline.description_html: Pre-computed view: rulemakings with both proposed and final rules, showing the timeline from proposal to finalization.

comments_monthly.description_html: Pre-computed view: monthly comment volume per agency.

agency_overview.description_html: Pre-computed view: summary statistics per agency including docket, document, and comment counts.

top_submitters.description_html: Pre-computed view: organizations submitting 5+ comments, ranked by volume.

cfr_sections.description_html: Current Code of Federal Regulations (eCFR) — the actual text of federal regulations in force. Covers 19 CFR titles with 123,000+ regulatory sections and full-text search.

congressional_record.description_html: Congressional Record — full text of everything said on the floor of Congress. Speeches, debates, procedural actions from 1994 to present. House, Senate, Extensions of Remarks, and Daily Digest.

crec_speakers.description_html: Normalized speaker data from the Congressional Record. Links members of Congress to their floor speeches, remarks, and procedural actions. 1,800+ unique speakers.

crec_bills.description_html: Bills referenced in Congressional Record floor proceedings. Links floor debate to specific legislation. Cross-references with the legislation table via bill_id.

congress_members.description_html: All current and historical members of Congress (12,700+) from the congress-legislators project. Includes bioguide_id (universal key), party, state, chamber, and cross-reference IDs for OpenSecrets, FEC, GovTrack, and more.

stock_trades.description_html: Congressional stock trading disclosures from 100% government sources. Senate: efdsearch.senate.gov (15,000+ transactions, 2012-present). House: disclosures-clerk.house.gov PTR PDFs parsed for transaction-level data (tickers, amounts, buy/sell). 64,000+ total transactions. Both chambers linked to congress_members via bioguide_id.

member_overview.description_html: Pre-computed view: current members of Congress with stock trade counts, floor speech counts, and bills sponsored — the big picture of each member's activity.

stock_trades_by_ticker.description_html: Pre-computed view: aggregate stock trading activity per ticker symbol, showing which stocks are most traded by members of Congress.

committees.description_html: Congressional committees and subcommittees. Links to committee memberships.

committee_memberships.description_html: Current committee membership for all members of Congress. Includes titles (Chairman, Ranking Member) and party affiliation. Linked to congress_members via bioguide_id.

legislation_cosponsors.description_html: Cosponsors for tracked legislation. Distinguishes original cosponsors (signed on at introduction) from later additions. Linked to congress_members via bioguide_id.

committee_member_trades.description_html: Pre-computed view: cross-reference of committee memberships with stock trades, showing what committee members are trading.

roll_call_votes.description_html: Roll call votes in the House and Senate, Congresses 106–119. Includes vote question, result, and linked bill.

member_votes.description_html: Individual member voting records for 8.3M+ votes. Links to roll_call_votes and congress_members via bioguide_id.

comment_details.description_html: Full comment text and metadata for a subset of Regulations.gov comments. Includes submitter type, organization, and attachment info.

fec_candidates.description_html: FEC candidate records — 64,000+ candidates for federal office with party, state, office, and election cycles.

fec_committees.description_html: FEC political committees — PACs, party committees, campaign committees. 155,000+ records.

fec_contributions.description_html: FEC contributions from committees to candidates. 4.4M+ records with amounts, dates, and election types.

fec_candidate_crosswalk.description_html: Maps FEC candidate IDs to bioguide_id, enabling cross-reference between campaign finance and congressional activity.

fec_employer_totals.description_html: Pre-computed: total individual contributions aggregated by employer name.

fec_employer_to_candidate.description_html: Pre-computed: individual contributions aggregated by employer → candidate.

fec_employer_to_party.description_html: Pre-computed: individual contributions aggregated by employer → party.

fec_top_occupations.description_html: Pre-computed: individual contributions aggregated by occupation.

fara_registrants.description_html: FARA foreign agent registrants — firms and individuals registered under the Foreign Agents Registration Act.

fara_foreign_principals.description_html: Foreign principals (governments, political parties, foreign entities) represented by FARA registrants. 17,000+ records with country of origin.

fara_short_forms.description_html: Individual foreign agents (employees/associates of registrants) registered under FARA. 44,000+ records.

fara_registrant_docs.description_html: Documents filed by FARA registrants — registration statements, supplemental statements, and informational materials. 151,000+ filings.

lobbying_filings.description_html: Senate Lobbying Disclosure Act filings (1999–2026). LD-1 registrations and LD-2 quarterly activity reports. 1.9M+ filings.

lobbying_activities.description_html: Lobbying activity reports by issue area. Each row is one issue area within a quarterly filing, with government entities contacted and description of lobbying activities.

lobbying_lobbyists.description_html: Individual lobbyists listed on LDA filings. 4.4M+ records with covered positions (former government roles).

lobbying_issue_codes.description_html: Reference table: 79 lobbying issue area codes (AGR, ENV, TAX, etc.) used in lobbying activity reports.

lobbying_contributions.description_html: LD-203 political contribution reports filed by lobbyists. Currently being populated.

docket_summary.description_html: Pre-computed: per-docket summary with document and comment counts, date ranges.

federal_register_agencies.description_html: Junction table linking Federal Register documents to their publishing agencies.

lis_to_bioguide.description_html: Crosswalk mapping Senate LIS IDs to bioguide_id for 326 senators.

hearings.description_html: Committee hearing transcripts from GovInfo (1993–present). Includes committee info, witness testimony, and member attendance.

columns.committees: JSON array of committee names and hierarchies.

hearing_witnesses.description_html: Witnesses who testified at committee hearings, with parsed name, title, organization, and location.

crs_reports.description_html: Congressional Research Service reports with summaries, authors, and topic classifications.

crs_report_bills.description_html: Cross-references between CRS reports and related legislation. Links CRS analysis to specific bills tracked in the legislation table.

nominations.description_html: Executive nominations to the Senate (Congresses 100–119), with confirmation status and vote tallies.

columns.status: Confirmed, Withdrawn, Returned, Rejected, or Pending.
columns.is_civilian: Whether this is a civilian (non-military) nomination.
columns.vote_yea: Yea votes if confirmed by roll call.
columns.vote_nay: Nay votes if confirmed by roll call.

nomination_actions.description_html: Action history for executive nominations (committee referral, hearings, votes).

treaties.description_html: Treaties submitted to the Senate (Congresses 89–119), with countries, index terms, and resolution text.

gao_reports.description_html: Government Accountability Office reports (1994–2008) with abstracts, legal references, and subject classifications.

cbo_cost_estimates.description_html: CBO cost estimates linked to legislation, extracted from Congress.gov bill data.

most_commented_dockets.title: Most Commented Dockets
most_commented_dockets.description: Dockets with the most public comments, showing comment volume and submitter breakdown.
most_commented_dockets.sql: SELECT docket_id, docket_title, agency_id, comment_count, org_comments, individual_comments, anonymous_comments, first_comment, last_comment FROM top_dockets ORDER BY comment_count DESC LIMIT 100

search_comments.title: Search Comments (Full Text)
search_comments.description: Search comment titles and submitter names using full-text search. Use standard search syntax: quotes for exact phrases, OR for alternatives.
search_comments.sql: SELECT comments.id, comments.agency_id, comments.title, comments.submitter_name, comments.submitter_type, comments.posted_date, comments.docket_id FROM comments_fts JOIN comments ON comments.rowid = comments_fts.rowid WHERE comments_fts MATCH :search ORDER BY rank LIMIT 200

search_federal_register.title: Search Federal Register
search_federal_register.description: Full-text search across Federal Register document titles, abstracts, and agency names.
search_federal_register.sql: SELECT federal_register.document_number, federal_register.title, federal_register.type, federal_register.publication_date, federal_register.agency_names, federal_register.html_url FROM federal_register_fts JOIN federal_register ON federal_register.rowid = federal_register_fts.rowid WHERE federal_register_fts MATCH :search ORDER BY rank LIMIT 200

search_dockets.title: Search Dockets
search_dockets.description: Full-text search across docket titles. Find regulatory proceedings by keyword.
search_dockets.sql: SELECT dockets.id, dockets.agency_id, dockets.title, dockets.docket_type, dockets.last_modified, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments WHERE docket_id = dockets.id) AS comment_count FROM dockets_fts JOIN dockets ON dockets.rowid = dockets_fts.rowid WHERE dockets_fts MATCH :search ORDER BY rank LIMIT 200

search_documents.title: Search Regulatory Documents
search_documents.description: Full-text search across regulatory document titles from Regulations.gov.
search_documents.sql: SELECT documents.id, documents.agency_id, documents.title, documents.document_type, documents.posted_date, documents.docket_id, documents.comment_end_date FROM documents_fts JOIN documents ON documents.rowid = documents_fts.rowid WHERE documents_fts MATCH :search ORDER BY rank LIMIT 200

open_for_comment.title: Currently Open for Comment
open_for_comment.description: Regulatory documents with comment periods that haven't closed yet. These are opportunities for public participation.
open_for_comment.sql: SELECT d.id, d.agency_id, d.title, d.document_type, d.posted_date, d.comment_start_date, d.comment_end_date, d.docket_id, CAST(julianday(d.comment_end_date) - julianday('now') AS INTEGER) AS days_remaining, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments c WHERE c.docket_id = d.docket_id) AS comments_so_far FROM documents d WHERE d.comment_end_date IS NOT NULL AND d.comment_end_date > datetime('now') AND d.withdrawn = 0 ORDER BY d.comment_end_date ASC LIMIT 100

agency_comment_trends.title: Comment Volume by Year & Agency
agency_comment_trends.description: Annual comment volume for each agency, with breakdown by submitter type.
agency_comment_trends.sql: SELECT agency_id, posted_year, comment_count, docket_count, org_comments, individual_comments, anonymous_comments FROM comments_by_year ORDER BY agency_id, posted_year

top_organizational_commenters.title: Most Active Organizational Commenters
top_organizational_commenters.description: Organizations that submit the most public comments across all agencies.
top_organizational_commenters.sql: SELECT submitter_name, comment_count, agencies_commented, dockets_commented, agency_list, first_comment, last_comment FROM top_submitters ORDER BY comment_count DESC LIMIT 100

rulemaking_pipeline.title: Rulemaking Pipeline (Proposed → Final)
rulemaking_pipeline.description: Dockets that have both proposed rules and final rules, showing the regulatory timeline.
rulemaking_pipeline.sql: SELECT d.id AS docket_id, d.agency_id, d.title, proposed.posted_date AS proposed_date, final.posted_date AS final_date, CAST(julianday(final.posted_date) - julianday(proposed.posted_date) AS INTEGER) AS days_to_finalize, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments c WHERE c.docket_id = d.id) AS total_comments FROM dockets d JOIN documents proposed ON proposed.docket_id = d.id AND proposed.document_type = 'Proposed Rule' JOIN documents final ON final.docket_id = d.id AND final.document_type = 'Rule' WHERE d.docket_type = 'Rulemaking' ORDER BY total_comments DESC LIMIT 100

comments_for_docket.title: Comments for a Docket
comments_for_docket.description: View all comments submitted for a specific docket ID (e.g., EPA-HQ-OAR-2021-0317).
comments_for_docket.sql: SELECT id, title, submitter_name, submitter_type, posted_date, agency_id FROM comments WHERE docket_id = :docket_id ORDER BY posted_date DESC LIMIT 500

dockets_for_agency.title: Dockets for an Agency
dockets_for_agency.description: Browse all dockets for a specific agency code (e.g., EPA, FDA, FWS, USDA) with comment counts.
dockets_for_agency.sql: SELECT d.id, d.title, d.docket_type, d.last_modified, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments c WHERE c.docket_id = d.id) AS comment_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM documents doc WHERE doc.docket_id = d.id) AS document_count FROM dockets d WHERE d.agency_id = :agency_id ORDER BY d.last_modified DESC LIMIT 200

fr_documents_for_agency.title: Federal Register Documents by Agency
fr_documents_for_agency.description: Search Federal Register documents by agency name and optional year.
fr_documents_for_agency.sql: SELECT document_number, title, type, publication_date, html_url FROM federal_register WHERE agency_names LIKE '%' || :agency || '%' AND (:year = '' OR pub_year = CAST(:year AS INTEGER)) ORDER BY publication_date DESC LIMIT 200

recent_rules.title: Recently Published Rules
recent_rules.description: The most recently published final rules in the Federal Register.
recent_rules.sql: SELECT document_number, title, publication_date, agency_names, html_url FROM federal_register WHERE type = 'Rule' ORDER BY publication_date DESC LIMIT 100

recently_proposed_rules.title: Recently Proposed Rules
recently_proposed_rules.description: Proposed rules awaiting public comment or finalization, ordered by publication date.
recently_proposed_rules.sql: SELECT document_number, title, publication_date, agency_names, html_url FROM federal_register WHERE type = 'Proposed Rule' ORDER BY publication_date DESC LIMIT 100

comment_window_analysis.title: Comment Period Durations
comment_window_analysis.description: How long agencies give the public to comment, by agency and document type.
comment_window_analysis.sql: SELECT d.agency_id, d.document_type, COUNT(*) AS documents, CAST(AVG(julianday(d.comment_end_date) - julianday(d.comment_start_date)) AS INTEGER) AS avg_days, CAST(MIN(julianday(d.comment_end_date) - julianday(d.comment_start_date)) AS INTEGER) AS min_days, CAST(MAX(julianday(d.comment_end_date) - julianday(d.comment_start_date)) AS INTEGER) AS max_days FROM documents d WHERE d.comment_start_date IS NOT NULL AND d.comment_end_date IS NOT NULL AND d.comment_start_date != '' AND d.comment_end_date != '' AND julianday(d.comment_end_date) > julianday(d.comment_start_date) GROUP BY d.agency_id, d.document_type HAVING documents >= 3 ORDER BY avg_days DESC
comment_window_analysis.description_html: How long agencies give the public to comment, by agency and document type. Useful for understanding whether comment periods are adequate.

comment_activity_monthly.title: Monthly Comment Activity
comment_activity_monthly.description: Comment volume by month across all agencies, useful for spotting surges in public engagement.
comment_activity_monthly.sql: SELECT year_month, agency_id, comment_count, active_dockets FROM comments_monthly ORDER BY year_month DESC LIMIT 500

cross_reference.title: Federal Register ↔ Regulations.gov Lookup
cross_reference.description: Find the Regulations.gov documents linked to a Federal Register document number, or vice versa.
cross_reference.sql: SELECT xr.fr_document_number, fr.title AS fr_title, fr.type AS fr_type, fr.publication_date, fr.html_url AS fr_url, xr.regs_document_id, doc.title AS regs_title, doc.document_type AS regs_type, doc.agency_id, doc.docket_id FROM fr_regs_crossref xr LEFT JOIN federal_register fr ON fr.document_number = xr.fr_document_number LEFT JOIN documents doc ON doc.id = xr.regs_document_id WHERE (:fr_doc_number = '' OR xr.fr_document_number = :fr_doc_number) AND (:regs_doc_id = '' OR xr.regs_document_id = :regs_doc_id) ORDER BY fr.publication_date DESC LIMIT 100

document_type_trends.title: Federal Register Activity by Year
document_type_trends.description: Count of Federal Register documents by type and year, showing regulatory activity trends over three decades.
document_type_trends.sql: SELECT pub_year, SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'Rule' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS rules, SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'Proposed Rule' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS proposed_rules, SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'Notice' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS notices, SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'Presidential Document' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS presidential, COUNT(*) AS total FROM federal_register WHERE pub_year IS NOT NULL GROUP BY pub_year ORDER BY pub_year

submitter_docket_detail.title: Comments by a Specific Submitter
submitter_docket_detail.description: See all comments from a specific organization or individual across all dockets.
submitter_docket_detail.sql: SELECT c.id, c.agency_id, c.docket_id, c.title, c.submitter_type, c.posted_date, d.title AS docket_title FROM comments c LEFT JOIN dockets d ON d.id = c.docket_id WHERE c.submitter_name = :submitter_name ORDER BY c.posted_date DESC LIMIT 200

executive_orders.title: Executive Orders
executive_orders.description: All Executive Orders from 1994 to present, with EO numbers and signing dates.
executive_orders.sql: SELECT executive_order_number AS eo_number, title, signing_date, publication_date, html_url FROM presidential_documents WHERE document_type = 'executive_order' AND executive_order_number IS NOT NULL ORDER BY executive_order_number DESC LIMIT 500

search_executive_orders.title: Search Executive Orders & Proclamations
search_executive_orders.description: Search presidential documents by keyword in title or abstract.
search_executive_orders.sql: SELECT document_type, executive_order_number AS eo_number, title, signing_date, publication_date, html_url, abstract FROM presidential_documents WHERE title LIKE '%' || :search || '%' OR abstract LIKE '%' || :search || '%' ORDER BY signing_date DESC LIMIT 200

recent_executive_orders.title: Recent Executive Orders
recent_executive_orders.description: The 50 most recently signed Executive Orders.
recent_executive_orders.sql: SELECT executive_order_number AS eo_number, title, signing_date, publication_date, html_url, abstract FROM presidential_documents WHERE document_type = 'executive_order' ORDER BY signing_date DESC LIMIT 50

executive_order_lookup.title: Look Up Executive Order by Number
executive_order_lookup.description: Find a specific Executive Order by its number (e.g., 14110).
executive_order_lookup.sql: SELECT executive_order_number AS eo_number, title, signing_date, publication_date, html_url, abstract FROM presidential_documents WHERE executive_order_number = CAST(:eo_number AS INTEGER)

presidential_documents_by_year.title: Presidential Documents by Year
presidential_documents_by_year.description: Count of Executive Orders and Proclamations signed each year.
presidential_documents_by_year.sql: SELECT CAST(strftime('%Y', signing_date) AS INTEGER) AS year, SUM(CASE WHEN document_type = 'executive_order' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS executive_orders, SUM(CASE WHEN document_type = 'proclamation' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS proclamations, COUNT(*) AS total FROM presidential_documents WHERE signing_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY year ORDER BY year DESC

proclamations.title: Presidential Proclamations
proclamations.description: All Presidential Proclamations, most recent first.
proclamations.sql: SELECT title, signing_date, publication_date, html_url FROM presidential_documents WHERE document_type = 'proclamation' ORDER BY signing_date DESC LIMIT 200

spending_by_agency.title: Federal Spending by Agency
spending_by_agency.description: Total grants and contracts by agency and fiscal year from USAspending.gov.
spending_by_agency.sql: SELECT agency, sub_agency, award_category, award_count, total_amount, avg_amount FROM spending_by_agency ORDER BY total_amount DESC

top_grant_recipients.title: Top Grant Recipients
top_grant_recipients.description: Organizations receiving the most federal grant money from our tracked agencies.
top_grant_recipients.sql: SELECT recipient_name, COUNT(*) AS grant_count, CAST(SUM(award_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_amount, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT COALESCE(sub_agency, agency)) AS agencies, MIN(start_date) AS earliest, MAX(start_date) AS latest FROM spending_awards WHERE award_category = 'grants' AND recipient_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY recipient_name ORDER BY total_amount DESC LIMIT 100

top_contractors.title: Top Contractors
top_contractors.description: Companies receiving the most federal contract money from our tracked agencies.
top_contractors.sql: SELECT recipient_name, COUNT(*) AS contract_count, CAST(SUM(award_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_amount, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT COALESCE(sub_agency, agency)) AS agencies FROM spending_awards WHERE award_category = 'contracts' AND recipient_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY recipient_name ORDER BY total_amount DESC LIMIT 100

search_spending.title: Search Spending Awards
search_spending.description: Search grants and contracts by description keyword.
search_spending.sql: SELECT award_id, COALESCE(sub_agency, agency) AS agency_name, award_category, recipient_name, CAST(award_amount AS INTEGER) AS amount, description, start_date, fiscal_year FROM spending_awards WHERE description LIKE '%' || :search || '%' ORDER BY award_amount DESC LIMIT 200

spending_by_state.title: Federal Spending by State
spending_by_state.description: Total awards by state of performance for our tracked agencies.
spending_by_state.sql: SELECT state_code, COUNT(*) AS award_count, CAST(SUM(award_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN award_category = 'grants' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS grants, SUM(CASE WHEN award_category = 'contracts' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS contracts FROM spending_awards WHERE state_code IS NOT NULL AND state_code != '' GROUP BY state_code ORDER BY total_amount DESC

search_legislation.title: Search Legislation
search_legislation.description: Search bills and resolutions by keyword in title or summary.
search_legislation.sql: SELECT bill_id, congress, bill_type || ' ' || bill_number AS bill, title, policy_area, introduced_date, latest_action_text, sponsor_name, cosponsor_count FROM legislation WHERE title LIKE '%' || :search || '%' OR summary_text LIKE '%' || :search || '%' ORDER BY introduced_date DESC LIMIT 200

legislation_by_policy_area.title: Legislation by Policy Area
legislation_by_policy_area.description: Browse bills filtered by CRS policy area (e.g., Environmental Protection, Health, Agriculture and Food, Animals).
legislation_by_policy_area.sql: SELECT bill_id, congress, UPPER(bill_type) || ' ' || bill_number AS bill, title, introduced_date, latest_action_text, sponsor_name, sponsor_party, cosponsor_count FROM legislation WHERE policy_area = :policy_area ORDER BY introduced_date DESC LIMIT 200

recent_legislation.title: Recently Introduced Legislation
recent_legislation.description: The most recently introduced bills in our tracked policy areas.
recent_legislation.sql: SELECT bill_id, congress, UPPER(bill_type) || ' ' || bill_number AS bill, title, policy_area, introduced_date, sponsor_name, sponsor_party, cosponsor_count, latest_action_text FROM legislation ORDER BY introduced_date DESC LIMIT 100

legislation_by_sponsor.title: Legislation by Sponsor
legislation_by_sponsor.description: Bills sponsored by a specific member of Congress.
legislation_by_sponsor.sql: SELECT bill_id, congress, UPPER(bill_type) || ' ' || bill_number AS bill, title, policy_area, introduced_date, latest_action_text, cosponsor_count FROM legislation WHERE sponsor_name LIKE '%' || :sponsor || '%' ORDER BY introduced_date DESC LIMIT 200

most_cosponsored_bills.title: Most Cosponsored Bills
most_cosponsored_bills.description: Bills with the most cosponsors, indicating broad congressional support.
most_cosponsored_bills.sql: SELECT bill_id, congress, UPPER(bill_type) || ' ' || bill_number AS bill, title, policy_area, introduced_date, sponsor_name, sponsor_party, cosponsor_count FROM legislation WHERE cosponsor_count > 0 ORDER BY cosponsor_count DESC LIMIT 100

legislation_subject_counts.title: Top Legislative Subjects
legislation_subject_counts.description: Most common CRS subject terms across all tracked legislation.
legislation_subject_counts.sql: SELECT subject, COUNT(*) AS bill_count FROM legislation_subjects GROUP BY subject ORDER BY bill_count DESC LIMIT 100

search_cfr.title: Search Federal Regulations (eCFR)
search_cfr.description: Full-text search across the Code of Federal Regulations. Find what the actual regulations say about any topic.
search_cfr.sql: SELECT cfr_sections.section_id, cfr_sections.title_number, cfr_sections.part_number, cfr_sections.section_number, cfr_sections.section_heading, cfr_sections.agency, cfr_sections.part_name, substr(cfr_sections.full_text, 1, 500) AS excerpt FROM cfr_fts JOIN cfr_sections ON cfr_sections.rowid = cfr_fts.rowid WHERE cfr_fts MATCH :search ORDER BY rank LIMIT 100

cfr_by_agency.title: Regulations by Agency
cfr_by_agency.description: Browse CFR sections by agency (EPA, FDA, APHIS, FWS, FSIS, NOAA).
cfr_by_agency.sql: SELECT section_id, title_number, part_number, section_number, section_heading, part_name, subpart_name, substr(full_text, 1, 300) AS excerpt FROM cfr_sections WHERE agency = :agency ORDER BY section_id LIMIT 200

cfr_parts.title: CFR Parts Overview
cfr_parts.description: List all parts (regulatory chapters) in each CFR title, with section counts.
cfr_parts.sql: SELECT title_number, title_name, part_number, part_name, agency, COUNT(*) AS section_count FROM cfr_sections WHERE part_number != '' GROUP BY title_number, part_number ORDER BY title_number, CAST(part_number AS INTEGER)

cfr_section_detail.title: Read a CFR Section
cfr_section_detail.description: View the full text of a specific CFR section (e.g., '1.1' or '50.11').
cfr_section_detail.sql: SELECT section_id, title_number, title_name, part_number, part_name, section_number, section_heading, agency, authority, source_citation, amendment_citations, full_text FROM cfr_sections WHERE section_number = :section_number

search_congressional_record.title: Search Congressional Record
search_congressional_record.description: Full-text search across everything said on the floor of Congress. Speeches, debates, procedural actions.
search_congressional_record.sql: SELECT congressional_record.granule_id, congressional_record.date, congressional_record.congress, congressional_record.chamber, congressional_record.title, congressional_record.speakers, congressional_record.page_start, substr(congressional_record.full_text, 1, 500) AS excerpt FROM crec_fts JOIN congressional_record ON congressional_record.rowid = crec_fts.rowid WHERE crec_fts MATCH :search ORDER BY rank LIMIT 100

congressional_record_by_date.title: Congressional Record by Date
congressional_record_by_date.description: View everything said on the floor of Congress on a specific date (YYYY-MM-DD).
congressional_record_by_date.sql: SELECT granule_id, chamber, granule_class, sub_granule_class, title, speakers, page_start, page_end FROM congressional_record WHERE date = :date ORDER BY chamber, page_start

floor_speeches_by_topic.title: Floor Speeches by Topic
floor_speeches_by_topic.description: Search House and Senate floor speeches (excludes Daily Digest and procedural items).
floor_speeches_by_topic.sql: SELECT congressional_record.granule_id, congressional_record.date, congressional_record.chamber, congressional_record.title, congressional_record.speakers, congressional_record.page_start, substr(congressional_record.full_text, 1, 500) AS excerpt FROM crec_fts JOIN congressional_record ON congressional_record.rowid = crec_fts.rowid WHERE crec_fts MATCH :search AND congressional_record.granule_class IN ('HOUSE', 'SENATE') AND congressional_record.sub_granule_class = 'ALLOTHER' ORDER BY rank LIMIT 100

congressional_record_stats.title: Congressional Record by Congress
congressional_record_stats.description: Volume of floor activity per Congress — speeches, proceedings, and extensions of remarks.
congressional_record_stats.sql: SELECT congress, chamber, COUNT(*) AS entries, COUNT(DISTINCT date) AS session_days FROM congressional_record WHERE congress IS NOT NULL GROUP BY congress, chamber ORDER BY congress DESC, chamber

speeches_by_member.title: Floor Speeches by Member of Congress
speeches_by_member.description: All floor speeches and remarks by a specific member of Congress. Enter a last name (e.g., 'McConnell'), full name, or bioguide_id.
speeches_by_member.sql: SELECT cs.speaker_name, cs.bioguide_id, cm.full_name AS official_name, cm.party, cr.date, cr.title, cr.chamber, cr.granule_class, cr.page_start, substr(cr.full_text, 1, 400) AS excerpt FROM crec_speakers cs JOIN congressional_record cr ON cs.granule_id = cr.granule_id LEFT JOIN congress_members cm ON cs.bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id WHERE cs.speaker_name LIKE '%' || :name || '%' OR cs.bioguide_id = :name ORDER BY cr.date DESC LIMIT 200

most_active_speakers.title: Most Active Speakers in Congress
most_active_speakers.description: Members of Congress with the most floor speeches across all Congresses on record.
most_active_speakers.sql: SELECT speaker_name, bioguide_id, official_name, party, state, total_speeches, active_days, first_appearance, last_appearance, chambers FROM speaker_activity ORDER BY total_speeches DESC LIMIT 100

bills_discussed_on_floor.title: Bills Discussed on the Floor
bills_discussed_on_floor.description: Bills referenced in Congressional Record floor proceedings, showing how many times each was discussed. Can filter by bill type (hr, s, hjres, etc.).
bills_discussed_on_floor.sql: SELECT bill_id, bill_type, bill_number, congress, COALESCE(bill_title, '(not in legislation table)') AS bill_title, floor_mentions AS mentions, first_discussed, last_discussed FROM bills_floor_time WHERE congress = :congress AND (:bill_type = '' OR bill_type = :bill_type) ORDER BY floor_mentions DESC LIMIT 100

floor_debate_for_bill.title: Floor Debate for a Specific Bill
floor_debate_for_bill.description: All Congressional Record entries referencing a specific bill. Enter bill_id format: congress-type-number (e.g., '118-hr-3684').
floor_debate_for_bill.sql: SELECT cr.date, cr.title, cr.chamber, (SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(speaker_name, '; ') FROM crec_speakers WHERE granule_id = cr.granule_id) AS speakers, cr.page_start, substr(cr.full_text, 1, 400) AS excerpt FROM crec_bills cb JOIN congressional_record cr ON cb.granule_id = cr.granule_id WHERE cb.bill_id = :bill_id ORDER BY cr.date

search_members.title: Search Congress Members
search_members.description: Find current or historical members of Congress by name.
search_members.sql: SELECT bioguide_id, full_name, party, state, chamber, CASE WHEN is_current THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS current, first_served, last_served, opensecrets_id, wikipedia_id FROM congress_members WHERE full_name LIKE '%' || :name || '%' OR last_name LIKE '%' || :name || '%' ORDER BY is_current DESC, last_served DESC LIMIT 200

member_profile.title: Member Profile: Trades, Speeches, Bills
member_profile.description: Complete profile for a member of Congress — their stock trades, floor speeches, and sponsored bills. Enter a bioguide_id (e.g., G000386 for Chuck Grassley).
member_profile.sql: SELECT cm.full_name, cm.party, cm.state, cm.chamber, cm.first_served, cm.last_served, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM stock_trades WHERE bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id) AS total_trades, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM crec_speakers WHERE bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id) AS total_speeches, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM legislation WHERE sponsor_bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id) AS bills_sponsored, (SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ticker, ', ') FROM stock_trades WHERE bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id AND ticker != '' LIMIT 20) AS tickers_traded FROM congress_members cm WHERE cm.bioguide_id = :bioguide_id

stock_trades_for_member.title: Stock Trades for a Member
stock_trades_for_member.description: All stock trading disclosures for a specific member. Enter a name (e.g., 'Pelosi') or bioguide_id.
stock_trades_for_member.sql: SELECT st.member_name, st.transaction_date, st.ticker, st.asset_description, st.transaction_type, st.amount_range, st.owner, st.chamber, st.source_url FROM stock_trades st WHERE st.member_name LIKE '%' || :name || '%' OR st.bioguide_id = :name ORDER BY st.transaction_date DESC LIMIT 500

most_active_traders.title: Most Active Congressional Traders
most_active_traders.description: Members of Congress with the most stock trading disclosures.
most_active_traders.sql: SELECT st.member_name, cm.party, cm.state, cm.chamber, COUNT(*) AS trade_count, COUNT(DISTINCT st.ticker) AS unique_tickers, SUM(CASE WHEN st.transaction_type LIKE '%Purchase%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS purchases, SUM(CASE WHEN st.transaction_type LIKE '%Sale%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS sales, MIN(st.transaction_date) AS first_trade, MAX(st.transaction_date) AS last_trade FROM stock_trades st LEFT JOIN congress_members cm ON st.bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id WHERE st.chamber = 'Senate' AND st.ticker IS NOT NULL AND st.ticker != '' GROUP BY st.member_name ORDER BY trade_count DESC LIMIT 100

most_traded_stocks.title: Most Traded Stocks by Congress
most_traded_stocks.description: Stocks most frequently bought or sold by members of Congress (both chambers).
most_traded_stocks.sql: SELECT ticker, asset_description, trade_count, trader_count, purchases, sales, traders, first_trade, last_trade FROM stock_trades_by_ticker ORDER BY trade_count DESC LIMIT 100

trades_by_ticker.title: Who Traded a Specific Stock?
trades_by_ticker.description: Find all members who traded a specific stock by ticker symbol (e.g., AAPL, MSFT).
trades_by_ticker.sql: SELECT member_name, transaction_date, transaction_type, amount_range, owner, asset_description, source_url FROM stock_trades WHERE UPPER(ticker) = UPPER(:ticker) ORDER BY transaction_date DESC

member_activity_timeline.title: Member Activity Timeline
member_activity_timeline.description: See a member's stock trades alongside their floor speeches. Enter bioguide_id.
member_activity_timeline.sql: SELECT 'trade' AS activity_type, st.transaction_date AS date, st.ticker || ' ' || st.transaction_type || ' (' || st.amount_range || ')' AS description, st.chamber FROM stock_trades st WHERE st.bioguide_id = :bioguide_id AND st.transaction_date != '' UNION ALL SELECT 'speech' AS activity_type, cr.date, cr.title AS description, cr.chamber FROM crec_speakers cs JOIN congressional_record cr ON cs.granule_id = cr.granule_id WHERE cs.bioguide_id = :bioguide_id ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 500

current_members_overview.title: Current Members of Congress Overview
current_members_overview.description: All current members with their trade count, speech count, and bills sponsored.
current_members_overview.sql: SELECT cm.bioguide_id, cm.full_name, cm.party, cm.state, cm.chamber, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM stock_trades WHERE bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id) AS trades, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM crec_speakers WHERE bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id) AS speeches, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM legislation WHERE sponsor_bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id) AS bills FROM congress_members cm WHERE cm.is_current = 1 ORDER BY trades DESC, speeches DESC

committee_members.title: Committee Members
committee_members.description: View all members of a specific committee. Enter a committee ID (e.g., SSEV for Senate Environment, HSAG for House Agriculture).
committee_members.sql: SELECT cm.bioguide_id, cm.member_name, cm.party, cm.title AS role, cm.rank, c.name AS committee_name, c.chamber, m.state, m.is_current FROM committee_memberships cm JOIN committees c ON cm.committee_id = c.committee_id LEFT JOIN congress_members m ON cm.bioguide_id = m.bioguide_id WHERE cm.committee_id = :committee_id ORDER BY cm.rank

member_committees.title: Committees for a Member
member_committees.description: See all committee assignments for a specific member. Enter bioguide_id.
member_committees.sql: SELECT c.committee_id, c.name, c.chamber, cm.title AS role, cm.rank, c.parent_committee_id, COALESCE(p.name, '') AS parent_committee FROM committee_memberships cm JOIN committees c ON cm.committee_id = c.committee_id LEFT JOIN committees p ON c.parent_committee_id = p.committee_id WHERE cm.bioguide_id = :bioguide_id ORDER BY c.chamber, c.name

committee_leadership.title: Committee Leadership
committee_leadership.description: Chairs and ranking members of all committees.
committee_leadership.sql: SELECT cm.title AS role, cm.member_name, cm.party, c.name AS committee, c.chamber, cm.bioguide_id FROM committee_memberships cm JOIN committees c ON cm.committee_id = c.committee_id WHERE cm.title IN ('Chairman', 'Ranking Member', 'Chair', 'Vice Chairman', 'Vice Chair') ORDER BY c.chamber, c.name, cm.title

committee_trade_activity.title: Stock Trades by Committee Members
committee_trade_activity.description: All stock trades by members of a specific committee. Enter committee ID.
committee_trade_activity.sql: SELECT cm.member_name, cm.title AS committee_role, st.ticker, st.transaction_type, st.transaction_date, st.amount_range, st.asset_description FROM committee_memberships cm JOIN stock_trades st ON cm.bioguide_id = st.bioguide_id WHERE cm.committee_id = :committee_id AND st.ticker IS NOT NULL AND st.ticker != '' ORDER BY st.transaction_date DESC LIMIT 500

bill_cosponsors.title: Cosponsors for a Bill
bill_cosponsors.description: View all cosponsors for a specific bill. Enter bill_id format: congress-type-number (e.g., '118-hr-3684').
bill_cosponsors.sql: SELECT lc.bioguide_id, cm.full_name, lc.party, lc.state, CASE WHEN lc.is_original_cosponsor THEN 'Original' ELSE 'Added later' END AS cosponsor_type, lc.sponsorship_date, cm.chamber FROM legislation_cosponsors lc LEFT JOIN congress_members cm ON lc.bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id WHERE lc.bill_id = :bill_id ORDER BY lc.is_original_cosponsor DESC, lc.sponsorship_date

most_prolific_cosponsors.title: Most Prolific Cosponsors
most_prolific_cosponsors.description: Members who cosponsor the most bills in our tracked policy areas.
most_prolific_cosponsors.sql: SELECT lc.bioguide_id, cm.full_name, cm.party, cm.state, cm.chamber, COUNT(*) AS bills_cosponsored, SUM(CASE WHEN lc.is_original_cosponsor THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS original_cosponsors FROM legislation_cosponsors lc LEFT JOIN congress_members cm ON lc.bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id GROUP BY lc.bioguide_id ORDER BY bills_cosponsored DESC LIMIT 100

bipartisan_bills.title: Bipartisan Bills (Cross-Party Cosponsorship)
bipartisan_bills.description: Bills that have cosponsors from both parties, indicating bipartisan support.
bipartisan_bills.sql: SELECT l.bill_id, l.congress, UPPER(l.bill_type) || ' ' || l.bill_number AS bill, l.title, l.sponsor_name, l.sponsor_party, COUNT(DISTINCT lc.bioguide_id) AS total_cosponsors, SUM(CASE WHEN lc.party = 'R' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS r_cosponsors, SUM(CASE WHEN lc.party = 'D' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS d_cosponsors FROM legislation l JOIN legislation_cosponsors lc ON l.bill_id = lc.bill_id GROUP BY l.bill_id HAVING r_cosponsors > 0 AND d_cosponsors > 0 ORDER BY total_cosponsors DESC LIMIT 100

spending_by_sub_agency.title: Federal Spending by Sub-Agency
spending_by_sub_agency.description: Total grants and contracts broken down by sub-agency within each parent agency, with award counts and totals.
spending_by_sub_agency.sql: SELECT agency, sub_agency, award_category, COUNT(*) AS award_count, CAST(SUM(award_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_amount, CAST(AVG(award_amount) AS INTEGER) AS avg_amount, MIN(start_date) AS earliest, MAX(start_date) AS latest FROM spending_awards WHERE sub_agency IS NOT NULL AND sub_agency != '' GROUP BY agency, sub_agency, award_category ORDER BY total_amount DESC

spending_trends_over_time.title: Spending Trends Over Time
spending_trends_over_time.description: Federal spending by fiscal year and agency, showing how grant and contract funding changes year to year across all 20 tracked agencies.
spending_trends_over_time.sql: SELECT fiscal_year, COALESCE(sub_agency, agency) AS agency_name, COUNT(*) AS award_count, CAST(SUM(award_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN award_category = 'grants' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS grants, SUM(CASE WHEN award_category = 'contracts' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS contracts, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN award_category = 'grants' THEN award_amount ELSE 0 END) AS INTEGER) AS grant_total, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN award_category = 'contracts' THEN award_amount ELSE 0 END) AS INTEGER) AS contract_total FROM spending_awards WHERE fiscal_year IS NOT NULL GROUP BY fiscal_year, agency_name ORDER BY fiscal_year DESC, total_amount DESC

explore_member_search.title: Explore: Member Search
explore_member_search.description: Search Congress members by name with stock trade, speech, and bill counts.
explore_member_search.sql: SELECT cm.bioguide_id, cm.full_name, cm.party, cm.state, cm.chamber, cm.is_current, cm.first_served, cm.last_served, COALESCE(ms.trade_count, 0) AS trade_count, COALESCE(ms.speech_count, 0) AS speech_count, COALESCE(ms.bills_sponsored, 0) AS bills_sponsored FROM congress_members cm LEFT JOIN member_stats ms ON ms.bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id WHERE cm.full_name LIKE '%' || :name || '%' ORDER BY cm.is_current DESC, trade_count DESC LIMIT 50

explore_member_profile.title: Explore: Member Profile
explore_member_profile.description: Full member profile by bioguide_id with activity counts.
explore_member_profile.sql: SELECT cm.*, COALESCE(ms.trade_count, 0) AS trade_count, COALESCE(ms.speech_count, 0) AS speech_count, COALESCE(ms.bills_sponsored, 0) AS bills_sponsored FROM congress_members cm LEFT JOIN member_stats ms ON ms.bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id WHERE cm.bioguide_id = :bioguide_id

explore_member_trades.title: Explore: Member Stock Trades
explore_member_trades.description: Stock trades for a specific Congress member ordered by date.
explore_member_trades.sql: SELECT transaction_date, ticker, asset_description, transaction_type, amount_range, owner, source_url FROM stock_trades WHERE bioguide_id = :bioguide_id ORDER BY transaction_date DESC LIMIT 200

explore_member_speeches.title: Explore: Member Floor Speeches
explore_member_speeches.description: Congressional Record floor speeches for a specific member.
explore_member_speeches.sql: SELECT cr.granule_id, cr.date, cr.title, cr.chamber, SUBSTR(cr.full_text, 1, 300) AS excerpt FROM crec_speakers cs JOIN congressional_record cr ON cr.granule_id = cs.granule_id WHERE cs.bioguide_id = :bioguide_id ORDER BY cr.date DESC LIMIT 100

explore_member_bills.title: Explore: Member Legislation
explore_member_bills.description: Legislation sponsored by a specific Congress member.
explore_member_bills.sql: SELECT bill_id, congress, bill_type, bill_number, title, policy_area, introduced_date, cosponsor_count, latest_action_date, latest_action_text FROM legislation WHERE sponsor_bioguide_id = :bioguide_id ORDER BY introduced_date DESC LIMIT 100

explore_docket_detail.title: Explore: Docket Detail
explore_docket_detail.description: Docket information with document and comment counts.
explore_docket_detail.sql: SELECT d.*, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments WHERE docket_id = d.id) AS comment_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM documents WHERE docket_id = d.id) AS doc_count FROM dockets d WHERE d.id = :docket_id

explore_docket_documents.title: Explore: Docket Documents
explore_docket_documents.description: Documents in a specific regulatory docket ordered by date.
explore_docket_documents.sql: SELECT id, title, document_type, posted_date, comment_start_date, comment_end_date, fr_doc_num FROM documents WHERE docket_id = :docket_id ORDER BY posted_date DESC LIMIT 100

explore_docket_comments.title: Explore: Docket Comment Stats
explore_docket_comments.description: Comment statistics for a docket grouped by submitter type.
explore_docket_comments.sql: SELECT submitter_type, COUNT(*) AS comment_count, MIN(posted_date) AS first_comment, MAX(posted_date) AS last_comment FROM comments WHERE docket_id = :docket_id GROUP BY submitter_type ORDER BY comment_count DESC

explore_lobbying_client.title: Explore: Lobbying Client Search
explore_lobbying_client.description: Search lobbying clients by name with total spending and firm counts.
explore_lobbying_client.sql: SELECT client_name, SUM(income_amount) AS total_income, COUNT(DISTINCT registrant_name) AS firm_count, COUNT(*) AS filing_count, COUNT(DISTINCT issue_code) AS issue_count, MIN(filing_year) AS year_from, MAX(filing_year) AS year_to FROM lobbying_activities WHERE client_name LIKE '%' || :name || '%' GROUP BY client_name ORDER BY total_income DESC LIMIT 50

explore_lobbying_activity.title: Explore: Client Lobbying Activity
explore_lobbying_activity.description: Lobbying activity records for a specific client.
explore_lobbying_activity.sql: SELECT registrant_name, filing_year, filing_period, issue_code, income_amount, specific_issues, government_entities FROM lobbying_activities WHERE client_name = :client_name ORDER BY filing_year DESC, filing_period DESC LIMIT 100

explore_lobbying_lobbyists.title: Explore: Client Lobbyists
explore_lobbying_lobbyists.description: Lobbyists working for a specific client, including covered government positions.
explore_lobbying_lobbyists.sql: SELECT DISTINCT ll.lobbyist_name, ll.covered_position FROM lobbying_lobbyists ll JOIN lobbying_activities la ON la.filing_uuid = ll.filing_uuid WHERE la.client_name = :client_name ORDER BY ll.lobbyist_name LIMIT 100

explore_lobbying_issues.title: Explore: Lobbying Issue Areas
explore_lobbying_issues.description: All lobbying issue areas with filing counts and client counts.
explore_lobbying_issues.sql: SELECT issue_code, description, filing_count, client_count, total_income FROM lobbying_issue_summary ORDER BY filing_count DESC

explore_revolving_door.title: Explore: Revolving Door
explore_revolving_door.description: Lobbyists with prior government positions (covered positions) and their current clients.
explore_revolving_door.sql: SELECT ll.lobbyist_name, ll.covered_position, la.client_name, la.registrant_name, la.filing_year FROM lobbying_lobbyists ll JOIN lobbying_activities la ON la.filing_uuid = ll.filing_uuid WHERE ll.covered_position IS NOT NULL AND ll.covered_position != '' AND ll.covered_position != 'N/A' ORDER BY la.filing_year DESC LIMIT 200

fara_by_country.title: FARA: Foreign Principals by Country
fara_by_country.description: Foreign principals (governments, entities) grouped by country, ranked by number of active registrants.
fara_by_country.sql: SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS principals, COUNT(DISTINCT registration_number) AS registrants FROM fara_foreign_principals WHERE country IS NOT NULL GROUP BY country ORDER BY principals DESC LIMIT 50

fara_search_registrants.title: FARA: Search Registrants
fara_search_registrants.sql: SELECT registration_number, name, business_name, city, state, registration_date, termination_date FROM fara_registrants WHERE rowid IN (SELECT rowid FROM fara_registrants_fts WHERE fara_registrants_fts MATCH :search) ORDER BY name LIMIT 50

fara_agents_for_country.title: FARA: Agents Representing a Country
fara_agents_for_country.description: All registrants and agents representing foreign principals from a given country.
fara_agents_for_country.sql: SELECT fp.foreign_principal, fp.country, fp.registrant_name, fp.fp_registration_date FROM fara_foreign_principals fp WHERE UPPER(fp.country) LIKE '%' || UPPER(:country) || '%' ORDER BY fp.fp_registration_date DESC LIMIT 100

top_pac_donors_to_candidate.title: FEC: Top PAC Donors to a Candidate
top_pac_donors_to_candidate.description: Largest PAC/committee contributors to a specific member of Congress.
top_pac_donors_to_candidate.sql: SELECT fc.cmte_id, fc.cmte_name, SUM(fc.transaction_amt) AS total, COUNT(*) AS contributions, MIN(fc.cycle) AS first_cycle, MAX(fc.cycle) AS last_cycle FROM fec_contributions fc JOIN fec_candidate_crosswalk xw ON xw.fec_candidate_id = fc.cand_id WHERE xw.bioguide_id = :bioguide_id GROUP BY fc.cmte_id ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 50

top_employer_donors.title: FEC: Top Employer Aggregates
top_employer_donors.description: Employers whose employees contribute the most to federal candidates (aggregated from individual FEC filings).
top_employer_donors.sql: SELECT employer, donation_count, total_amount, avg_amount, unique_states FROM fec_employer_totals ORDER BY total_amount DESC LIMIT 50

employer_donations_to_candidate.title: FEC: Employer Donations to a Member
employer_donations_to_candidate.description: Top employers of individual donors to a specific member of Congress.
employer_donations_to_candidate.sql: SELECT employer, SUM(total_amount) AS total, SUM(donation_count) AS contributions FROM fec_employer_to_candidate WHERE bioguide_id = :bioguide_id GROUP BY employer ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 50

voting_record.title: Votes: Member Voting Record
voting_record.description: Recent roll call votes for a specific member of Congress.
voting_record.sql: SELECT rv.date, rv.chamber, rv.question, rv.result, mv.vote_cast, rv.bill_id FROM member_votes mv JOIN roll_call_votes rv ON rv.congress = mv.congress AND rv.chamber = mv.chamber AND rv.session = mv.session AND rv.roll_call_number = mv.roll_call_number WHERE mv.bioguide_id = :bioguide_id ORDER BY rv.date DESC LIMIT 100

party_line_votes.title: Votes: Most Partisan Roll Calls
party_line_votes.description: Roll call votes with the biggest party-line splits (most one-sided by party).
party_line_votes.sql: SELECT rv.date, rv.chamber, rv.question, rv.result, rv.bill_id, rv.yea_count, rv.nay_count FROM roll_call_votes rv WHERE rv.date >= '2023-01-01' ORDER BY rv.date DESC LIMIT 100

recent_votes.title: Votes: Recent Roll Calls
recent_votes.description: Most recent roll call votes across both chambers.
recent_votes.sql: SELECT date, chamber, question, result, bill_id FROM roll_call_votes ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 50

nomination_confirmation_rates.title: Nomination Confirmation Rates by Congress
nomination_confirmation_rates.description: Confirmation, withdrawal, return, and rejection rates for presidential nominations by Congress.
nomination_confirmation_rates.sql: SELECT congress, COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Confirmed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS confirmed, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Withdrawn' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS withdrawn, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Returned' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS returned, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Rejected' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS rejected, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'Confirmed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS confirm_pct FROM nominations GROUP BY congress ORDER BY congress

top_hearing_witnesses.title: Most Frequent Hearing Witnesses
top_hearing_witnesses.description: Witnesses who have appeared at the most congressional hearings, with their organization and date range.
top_hearing_witnesses.sql: SELECT hw.name, hw.organization, COUNT(DISTINCT hw.package_id) AS hearings_count, MIN(h.date_issued) AS first_appearance, MAX(h.date_issued) AS last_appearance FROM hearing_witnesses hw JOIN hearings h ON hw.package_id = h.package_id WHERE hw.name != '' GROUP BY hw.name HAVING hearings_count >= 3 ORDER BY hearings_count DESC LIMIT 100

hearings_by_committee.title: Committee Hearing Activity
hearings_by_committee.description: Number of hearings held by each chamber and Congress, showing committee activity levels over time.
hearings_by_committee.sql: SELECT chamber, congress, COUNT(*) AS hearing_count, MIN(date_issued) AS earliest, MAX(date_issued) AS latest FROM hearings GROUP BY chamber, congress ORDER BY congress DESC, chamber

gao_reports_by_subject.title: GAO Reports by Subject Area
gao_reports_by_subject.description: Government Accountability Office reports grouped by subject area, ranked by volume.
gao_reports_by_subject.sql: SELECT subjects, COUNT(*) AS report_count, MIN(date_issued) AS earliest, MAX(date_issued) AS latest FROM gao_reports WHERE subjects IS NOT NULL AND subjects != '' GROUP BY subjects ORDER BY report_count DESC LIMIT 100

crs_reports_by_topic.title: CRS Reports by Topic
crs_reports_by_topic.description: Congressional Research Service reports grouped by topic, with status breakdown.
crs_reports_by_topic.sql: SELECT topics, COUNT(*) AS report_count, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT status) AS statuses FROM crs_reports WHERE topics IS NOT NULL AND topics != '' GROUP BY topics ORDER BY report_count DESC LIMIT 100

recent_confirmed_nominations.title: Recently Confirmed Nominations
recent_confirmed_nominations.description: The most recently confirmed presidential nominations, including vote tallies.
recent_confirmed_nominations.sql: SELECT n.citation, n.description, n.organization, n.received_date, n.status, n.vote_yea, n.vote_nay FROM nominations n WHERE n.status = 'Confirmed' ORDER BY n.received_date DESC LIMIT 100

treaties_by_country.title: Treaties by Country
treaties_by_country.description: Treaties grouped by country, showing topic areas and date ranges.
treaties_by_country.sql: SELECT countries, COUNT(*) AS treaty_count, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT topic) AS topics, MIN(transmitted_date) AS earliest, MAX(transmitted_date) AS latest FROM treaties WHERE countries IS NOT NULL GROUP BY countries ORDER BY treaty_count DESC

witness_lobbyist_overlap.title: Hearing Witnesses Who Also Lobby Congress
witness_lobbyist_overlap.description: Organizations that testified as expert witnesses at congressional hearings while also paying lobbyists. Shows the intersection of public testimony and private influence.
witness_lobbyist_overlap.sql: SELECT * FROM witness_lobby_overlap ORDER BY hearings_testified DESC

commenter_lobbyist_overlap.title: Organizations That Comment on Regulations AND Lobby
commenter_lobbyist_overlap.description: Organizations that submit public comments on regulations while also paying lobbyists — showing dual-channel influence on the same regulatory process.
commenter_lobbyist_overlap.sql: SELECT * FROM commenter_lobby_overlap ORDER BY comments_filed DESC

committee_trading_activity.title: Stock Trading by Committee Members
committee_trading_activity.description: Members of Congress trading stocks while serving on committees that may oversee related industries. Shows trade counts, tickers, and committee roles.
committee_trading_activity.sql: SELECT cm.full_name, cm.party, cm.state, c.name AS committee_name, cmem.title AS committee_role, COUNT(DISTINCT st.id) AS total_trades, COUNT(DISTINCT st.ticker) AS unique_tickers, SUM(CASE WHEN st.transaction_type LIKE '%Purchase%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS purchases, SUM(CASE WHEN st.transaction_type LIKE '%Sale%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS sales, MIN(st.transaction_date) AS first_trade, MAX(st.transaction_date) AS last_trade FROM committee_memberships cmem JOIN committees c ON cmem.committee_id = c.committee_id JOIN congress_members cm ON cmem.bioguide_id = cm.bioguide_id JOIN stock_trades st ON cmem.bioguide_id = st.bioguide_id WHERE st.ticker IS NOT NULL AND st.ticker != '' GROUP BY cmem.bioguide_id, cmem.committee_id HAVING total_trades >= 10 ORDER BY total_trades DESC

top_donors_to_committees.title: Top Campaign Donors to Committee Members
top_donors_to_committees.description: Largest campaign contributions to current committee members, showing which PACs and organizations fund members of specific committees.
top_donors_to_committees.sql: SELECT committee_name, member_name, party, state, donor_committee, total_donated, contribution_count FROM committee_donor_summary ORDER BY total_donated DESC LIMIT 200

speeches_near_trades.title: Floor Speeches Within 7 Days of Stock Trades
speeches_near_trades.description: Members who gave floor speeches mentioning specific bills within 7 days of making stock trades — a potential indicator of trading on legislative knowledge.
speeches_near_trades.sql: SELECT full_name, party, state, trade_date, ticker, asset_description, transaction_type, amount_range, speech_date, speech_title, days_apart FROM speeches_near_trades ORDER BY full_name, trade_date DESC LIMIT 500

most_lobbied_bills.title: Most Lobbied Bills
most_lobbied_bills.description: Bills referenced most often in lobbying disclosure filings, showing which legislation attracts the most lobbying activity.
most_lobbied_bills.sql: SELECT bill_id, bill_title, policy_area, lobby_filings, unique_clients, issue_codes FROM lobbying_bill_summary ORDER BY lobby_filings DESC LIMIT 200

committee_trade_conflicts.title: Stock Trades in Committee-Regulated Sectors
committee_trade_conflicts.description: Members of Congress trading stocks that fall within the sectors regulated by their committee assignments — potential conflicts of interest.
committee_trade_conflicts.sql: SELECT full_name, party, state, committee_name, jurisdiction_desc, jurisdiction_tier, sic_code, sic_description, transaction_date, ticker, asset_description, transaction_type, amount_range FROM committee_trade_conflicts ORDER BY full_name, transaction_date DESC LIMIT 500

revolving_door.title: The Revolving Door: Former Members Who Became Lobbyists
revolving_door.description: Former members of Congress who registered as lobbyists, matched via name and covered-position disclosures in lobbying filings. Shows filing counts, clients, firms, and years active.
revolving_door.sql: SELECT full_name, party, state, congress_chamber, lobbying_filing_count, client_count, firm_count, first_lobbying_year, last_lobbying_year, total_reported_income, lobbying_firms, covered_position_sample FROM revolving_door ORDER BY lobbying_filing_count DESC LIMIT 300

committee_sector_map.title: Committee Jurisdiction → Regulated Sectors
committee_sector_map.description: Reference table mapping each congressional committee to the industries, tickers, and GICS/SIC sectors under its jurisdiction.
committee_sector_map.sql: SELECT committee_id, committee_name, chamber, regulated_sectors, example_tickers, gics_sectors, sic_ranges FROM committee_sectors ORDER BY chamber, committee_name

lobbying_issue_agency_map.title: Lobbying Issue Codes → Agencies & Committees
lobbying_issue_agency_map.description: Reference table mapping each lobbying disclosure issue code to the federal agencies and congressional committees with jurisdiction.
lobbying_issue_agency_map.sql: SELECT issue_code, issue_description, primary_agencies, regs_gov_agency_prefixes, related_committee_ids, notes FROM lobbying_issue_agencies ORDER BY issue_code

facilities.description_html: USDA APHIS-licensed animal welfare facilities including breeders, dealers, exhibitors, and research labs. 15,000+ facilities across all 50 states.

inspections.description_html: APHIS inspection records for licensed facilities. 110,000+ inspections from 2014–present with violation counts and links to official reports.

enforcement_actions.description_html: Formal enforcement actions (fines, suspensions, revocations) taken against APHIS-licensed facilities.

teachable_moments.description_html: Teachable moment citations issued during APHIS inspections, categorized by type.

annual_reports.description_html: Annual animal usage reports submitted by research facilities, broken down by species and USDA pain categories (C, D, E).

search_facilities.title: Search Facilities
search_facilities.description: Full-text search across facility names, cities, and states.
search_facilities.sql: SELECT f.cert_number, f.legal_name, f.dba_name, f.site_city, f.site_state, f.license_type, f.status, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM inspections i WHERE i.cert_number = f.cert_number) AS inspection_count FROM facilities_fts JOIN facilities f ON f.rowid = facilities_fts.rowid WHERE facilities_fts MATCH :search ORDER BY rank LIMIT 200

facility_inspections.title: Inspection History for a Facility
facility_inspections.description: View all inspections for a specific facility by certificate number (e.g., 43-A-0001).
facility_inspections.sql: SELECT i.inspection_date, i.inspection_type, i.critical_count, i.noncritical_count, i.direct_count, i.teachable_moment_count, f.legal_name, f.site_city, f.site_state FROM inspections i JOIN facilities f ON f.cert_number = i.cert_number WHERE i.cert_number = :cert_number ORDER BY i.inspection_date DESC

facilities_by_state.title: Facilities by State
facilities_by_state.description: Count of APHIS-licensed facilities in each state, broken down by license type.
facilities_by_state.sql: SELECT site_state, license_type, COUNT(*) AS facility_count FROM facilities WHERE site_state <> '' GROUP BY site_state, license_type ORDER BY site_state, facility_count DESC

recent_inspections.title: Recent Inspections
recent_inspections.description: The most recent APHIS inspections across all facilities.
recent_inspections.sql: SELECT i.inspection_date, i.cert_number, f.legal_name, f.site_city, f.site_state, i.inspection_type, i.critical_count, i.noncritical_count, i.direct_count FROM inspections i JOIN facilities f ON f.cert_number = i.cert_number ORDER BY i.inspection_date DESC LIMIT 200

critical_violations.title: Inspections with Critical Violations
critical_violations.description: Inspections where critical (serious) violations were found.
critical_violations.sql: SELECT i.inspection_date, i.cert_number, f.legal_name, f.site_city, f.site_state, f.license_type, i.critical_count, i.noncritical_count, i.direct_count FROM inspections i JOIN facilities f ON f.cert_number = i.cert_number WHERE i.critical_count > 0 ORDER BY i.inspection_date DESC LIMIT 200

worst_violators.title: Facilities with Most Violations
worst_violators.description: Facilities ranked by total critical + noncritical + direct violations across all inspections.
worst_violators.sql: SELECT f.cert_number, f.legal_name, f.site_city, f.site_state, f.license_type, COUNT(*) AS total_inspections, SUM(i.critical_count) AS total_critical, SUM(i.noncritical_count) AS total_noncritical, SUM(i.direct_count) AS total_direct, SUM(i.critical_count) + SUM(i.noncritical_count) + SUM(i.direct_count) AS total_violations FROM inspections i JOIN facilities f ON f.cert_number = i.cert_number GROUP BY i.cert_number HAVING total_violations > 0 ORDER BY total_violations DESC LIMIT 100

facilities_by_type.title: Facilities by License Type
facilities_by_type.description: Browse facilities filtered by license type (e.g., Class A - Breeder, Class C - Exhibitor, Class B - Dealer, Class R - Research Facility).
facilities_by_type.sql: SELECT cert_number, legal_name, dba_name, site_city, site_state, status FROM facilities WHERE license_type = :license_type ORDER BY legal_name LIMIT 500

inspection_trends.title: Inspection Trends by Year
inspection_trends.description: Annual inspection counts with violation breakdowns.
inspection_trends.sql: SELECT CAST(strftime('%Y', inspection_date) AS INTEGER) AS year, COUNT(*) AS inspections, SUM(CASE WHEN critical_count > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS with_critical, SUM(critical_count) AS total_critical, SUM(noncritical_count) AS total_noncritical, SUM(direct_count) AS total_direct FROM inspections WHERE inspection_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY year ORDER BY year DESC

lobbying_filings_raw.description_html: Raw lobbying disclosure filings from the Senate Office of Public Records (SOPR). Each row is a single filing — registrations, quarterly reports, amendments, and terminations. Includes registrant and client names, reported income/expenses, filing period, and the full raw JSON from the API.

lobbying_registrations.description_html: Lobbying registrations (LD-1 forms) filed when a lobbying firm or organization first registers to lobby on behalf of a client. Includes effective dates and client details.

lobbying_activities.description_html: Individual lobbying activities reported in quarterly filings. Each row is one issue area for one client — includes the specific issues lobbied on, government entities contacted, and income/expense amounts.

lobbying_lobbyists.description_html: Individual lobbyists listed on lobbying filings. Includes covered positions (prior government roles) and whether the lobbyist is newly added to the filing.

lobbying_contributions.description_html: Political contributions reported by lobbyists on LD-203 forms. Includes contribution amounts, recipients, and dates — required disclosures for registered lobbyists.

lobbying_issue_codes.description_html: Reference table of lobbying issue area codes (e.g., ENV = Environment/Superfund, HCR = Health Issues, TAX = Taxation/Internal Revenue Code). Used in lobbying_activities.

lobbying_gov_entities.description_html: Government entities contacted during lobbying activities — Congress, executive agencies, and White House offices.

lobbying_filing_types.description_html: Reference table of lobbying filing types (registrations, quarterly reports, amendments, terminations).

search_lobbying_clients.title: Search Lobbying Clients
search_lobbying_clients.description: Search for a client by name across all lobbying activity filings. Find who is lobbying on their behalf and on what issues.
search_lobbying_clients.sql: SELECT DISTINCT client_name, registrant_name, issue_code, filing_year, filing_period, CAST(income_amount AS INTEGER) AS income, government_entities, substr(specific_issues, 1, 300) AS issues_excerpt FROM lobbying_activities WHERE client_name LIKE '%' || :client_name || '%' ORDER BY filing_year DESC, filing_period DESC LIMIT 200

lobbying_by_issue_area.title: Lobbying by Issue Area
lobbying_by_issue_area.description: Count of lobbying activity filings by issue code, showing which policy areas attract the most lobbying.
lobbying_by_issue_area.sql: SELECT la.issue_code, COALESCE(lic.description, la.issue_code) AS issue_description, COUNT(*) AS filing_count, COUNT(DISTINCT la.client_name) AS unique_clients, COUNT(DISTINCT la.registrant_name) AS unique_registrants, CAST(SUM(la.income_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_income FROM lobbying_activities la LEFT JOIN lobbying_issue_codes lic ON la.issue_code = lic.code GROUP BY la.issue_code ORDER BY filing_count DESC

top_lobbying_clients_by_spending.title: Top Lobbying Clients by Spending
top_lobbying_clients_by_spending.description: Clients that spend the most on lobbying, ranked by total reported income across all filings and issue areas.
top_lobbying_clients_by_spending.sql: SELECT client_name, COUNT(*) AS activity_count, COUNT(DISTINCT registrant_name) AS firms_hired, COUNT(DISTINCT issue_code) AS issue_areas, CAST(SUM(income_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_reported, MIN(filing_year) AS first_year, MAX(filing_year) AS last_year FROM lobbying_activities WHERE income_amount > 0 GROUP BY client_name ORDER BY total_reported DESC LIMIT 100

top_lobbying_firms.title: Top Lobbying Firms (Registrants)
top_lobbying_firms.description: Lobbying firms (registrants) ranked by total reported income across all clients and filings.
top_lobbying_firms.sql: SELECT registrant_name, COUNT(*) AS activity_count, COUNT(DISTINCT client_name) AS unique_clients, COUNT(DISTINCT issue_code) AS issue_areas, CAST(SUM(income_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_income, MIN(filing_year) AS first_year, MAX(filing_year) AS last_year FROM lobbying_activities WHERE income_amount > 0 GROUP BY registrant_name ORDER BY total_income DESC LIMIT 100

lobbying_activity_for_client.title: Lobbying Activity for a Client
lobbying_activity_for_client.description: Detailed lobbying activity for a specific client — every issue area, government entity contacted, and specific issues lobbied on.
lobbying_activity_for_client.sql: SELECT la.filing_year, la.filing_period, la.registrant_name, la.issue_code, COALESCE(lic.description, la.issue_code) AS issue_description, la.government_entities, la.specific_issues, CAST(la.income_amount AS INTEGER) AS income, CAST(la.expense_amount AS INTEGER) AS expenses FROM lobbying_activities la LEFT JOIN lobbying_issue_codes lic ON la.issue_code = lic.code WHERE la.client_name = :client_name ORDER BY la.filing_year DESC, la.filing_period DESC LIMIT 500

lobbyists_for_client.title: Lobbyists for a Client
lobbyists_for_client.description: Individual lobbyists who have lobbied on behalf of a specific client, including their covered government positions.
lobbyists_for_client.sql: SELECT DISTINCT ll.lobbyist_name, ll.covered_position, la.registrant_name, la.client_name, la.filing_year, la.issue_code, COALESCE(lic.description, la.issue_code) AS issue_description FROM lobbying_lobbyists ll JOIN lobbying_activities la ON ll.filing_uuid = la.filing_uuid LEFT JOIN lobbying_issue_codes lic ON la.issue_code = lic.code WHERE la.client_name LIKE '%' || :client_name || '%' ORDER BY la.filing_year DESC, ll.lobbyist_name LIMIT 500

lobbying_spending_over_time.title: Lobbying Spending Over Time
lobbying_spending_over_time.description: Total reported lobbying income by year, showing spending trends across the lobbying industry.
lobbying_spending_over_time.sql: SELECT filing_year, COUNT(*) AS activity_count, COUNT(DISTINCT client_name) AS unique_clients, COUNT(DISTINCT registrant_name) AS unique_registrants, CAST(SUM(income_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_income, CAST(SUM(expense_amount) AS INTEGER) AS total_expenses FROM lobbying_activities WHERE filing_year IS NOT NULL GROUP BY filing_year ORDER BY filing_year DESC

search_specific_issues.title: Search Specific Issues
search_specific_issues.description: Full-text search on the specific issues described in lobbying filings. Find lobbying related to any topic, bill, or regulation.
search_specific_issues.sql: SELECT la.client_name, la.registrant_name, la.issue_code, COALESCE(lic.description, la.issue_code) AS issue_description, la.filing_year, la.filing_period, la.government_entities, la.specific_issues, CAST(la.income_amount AS INTEGER) AS income FROM lobbying_activities la LEFT JOIN lobbying_issue_codes lic ON la.issue_code = lic.code WHERE la.specific_issues LIKE '%' || :search || '%' ORDER BY la.filing_year DESC, la.income_amount DESC LIMIT 200

lobbyists_with_government_positions.title: Lobbyists with Government Positions
lobbyists_with_government_positions.description: Lobbyists who held covered government positions (revolving door) — former congressional staff, executive branch officials, and other government roles.
lobbyists_with_government_positions.sql: SELECT DISTINCT ll.lobbyist_name, ll.covered_position, la.registrant_name, la.client_name, la.filing_year FROM lobbying_lobbyists ll JOIN lobbying_activities la ON ll.filing_uuid = la.filing_uuid WHERE ll.covered_position IS NOT NULL AND ll.covered_position != '' ORDER BY ll.lobbyist_name, la.filing_year DESC LIMIT 500

political_contributions_by_lobbyists.title: Political Contributions by Lobbyists
political_contributions_by_lobbyists.description: Search political contributions reported by registered lobbyists. Enter a lobbyist name, recipient name, or leave blank for top contributions.
political_contributions_by_lobbyists.sql: SELECT lc.lobbyist_name, lc.contributor_name, lc.recipient_name, lc.payee_name, lc.contribution_type, CAST(lc.amount AS INTEGER) AS amount, lc.contribution_date, lc.filing_year FROM lobbying_contributions lc WHERE (:search = '' OR lc.lobbyist_name LIKE '%' || :search || '%' OR lc.recipient_name LIKE '%' || :search || '%' OR lc.contributor_name LIKE '%' || :search || '%') ORDER BY lc.amount DESC, lc.contribution_date DESC LIMIT 200

open_comments.title: Open for Comment — Daily Updated
open_comments.description_html: Documents currently open for public comment on Regulations.gov. Updated daily at 6:30 AM UTC. This is a small, frequently-refreshed database separate from the main OpenRegs archive.

documents.description_html: Federal regulatory documents currently open for public comment.

explore_open_comments.title: Explore: Open Comment Periods
explore_open_comments.description: Documents currently open for public comment, ordered by closing date.
explore_open_comments.sql: SELECT id, agency_id, title, document_type, comment_end_date, docket_id, CAST(julianday(comment_end_date) - julianday('now') AS INTEGER) AS days_left FROM documents WHERE comment_end_date > datetime('now') AND withdrawn = 0 ORDER BY comment_end_date ASC LIMIT 50
